_comment: Hand-authored from direct reading of BAC and Cowen PDFs. BAC is bull-side (BUY-rated); Cowen is bear-side (HOLD with valuation concern). Each bullet under 15 words and contains at least one AEIS whitelist token. No verbatim sentence-level quotes from the source PDFs.

bull_case_reasons: Semi sales +6% QoQ in Q2 with 2H ramping above 30% YoY into CY27
DCC outlook raised to mid-30s% for CY26 with 800V modules now sampling
eVoS, eVerest, and NavX driving semi share gains in memory and logic
WFE backdrop +23% in CY26 to $144bn and +27% in CY27 to $183bn
bac.key_debate_flagged: Whether 36x CY27 PE holds as DC and semi acceleration push EPS into CY28.
catalysts: 800V Kyber rack on Rubin in 2027 lifts content per system into CY28
I&M bookings +14% QoQ in Q1 with Q2 and Q3 catch-up expected
Thailand capacity adds $1bn, taking total above $3.5bn exiting CY26

bear_case_reasons: AEIS PE premium to AMAT and LRCX sits at 25% vs 5% three-year average
DC needs +40% growth from mid-30s% for the premium to be defensible
CY27 semi growth modeled at 22%, slightly below WFE growth of 27%
AMAT exposure to older tool mix can pressure margin into CY27
cowen.key_debate_flagged: Can AEIS hold a 25% PE premium to AMAT and LRCX without DC reaccelerating to 40%.
catalysts: Second wave of DC customers from enterprise and neocloud ramping in CY27
PECVD and dielectric etch share gains push CY27 semi growth above WFE
Q2 gross margin to 40.45% with guide ahead of Street consensus
